# ml-toolbox — Architecture Plan

> **Personal project.** Single-user, runs on a Mac Mini (local network only).
> No auth, no multi-tenancy, no SLA. Build it right, not enterprise-ready.

---

## Overview

This phase builds the complete foundation: node protocol, canvas UI, execution engine,
and sandbox infrastructure. No ML nodes yet — those come next, once the platform is solid.

Done correctly, any node following the `@node` protocol can be dropped into the canvas
and run safely in an isolated Docker container. The next phase is purely about writing
those nodes — no infrastructure changes required.

---

## Stack

```
Frontend:
  React 19
  TypeScript 5
  Vite 6
  Tailwind CSS v4           (@tailwindcss/vite plugin — no postcss needed)
  shadcn/ui                 (fully supports React 19 + Tailwind v4)
  @xyflow/react 12.10.1     (React Flow — package renamed from reactflow)
  @monaco-editor/react      (Python syntax highlighting + IntelliSense)
  pnpm

Backend:
  Python 3.13
  FastAPI 0.135.1           (fastapi[standard])
  uv
  Polars 1.39.0             (data processing between nodes)
  pandas 2.x                (final step — sklearn compatibility bridge)
  pyarrow 23.0.1            (Arrow/Parquet file format for node output storage)
  pydantic v2               (data validation, bundled with FastAPI)
  docker-py                 (Python Docker SDK — spawn sandbox containers)

Deployment:
  Docker Compose (Mac Mini — minyis-mac-mini, linux/arm64)
  Caddy 2                   (reverse proxy + static file server — frontend/API on one port)
  nginx                     (already configured: /ml-toolbox/ → localhost:8910)
  GHA: CI on PR (ruff format + pyright + pytest + eslint + tsc)
       CD on push to main (docker build linux/arm64 + SSH deploy via Tailscale)
  Port: 8910
  Vite base: "/ml-toolbox/"  (required so assets resolve correctly under subpath)
...

enable-cache: true              # cache keyed on uv.lock hash

      - name: Install deps
        run: uv sync --frozen

      - name: Ruff format check           # parallel Claude Code sessions can diverge
        run: uv run ruff format --check src/

      - name: Pyright
        run: uv run pyright src/

      - name: Pytest
        run: uv run pytest tests/ -x -q

  ci-frontend:
    name: Frontend
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: frontend
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml

      - name: Install deps
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: ESLint
        run: pnpm lint

      - name: TypeScript check
        run: pnpm tsc --noEmit
```

Estimated time: backend ~75s, frontend ~35s, both in parallel.

### CD

```yaml
# .github/workflows/cd.yml

name: CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  detect-changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      backend:  ${{ steps.filter.outputs.backend }}
      sandbox:  ${{ steps.filter.outputs.sandbox }}
      frontend: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.frontend }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            backend:
              - 'backend/src/**'
              - 'backend/pyproject.toml'
              - 'backend/uv.lock'
              - 'Dockerfile'
            sandbox:
              - 'backend/sandbox/**'
            frontend:
              - 'frontend/src/**'
              - 'frontend/package.json'
              - 'frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml'

  build-backend:
    name: Build backend
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.backend == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          file: Dockerfile
          push: true
          platforms: linux/arm64           # Mac Mini M4
          tags: ghcr.io/minyic7/ml-toolbox/backend:latest
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  build-sandbox:
    name: Build sandbox
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.sandbox == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: ./backend/sandbox
          push: true
          platforms: linux/arm64           # Mac Mini M4
          tags: ghcr.io/minyic7/ml-toolbox/sandbox:latest
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          # numpy/pyarrow/polars layer almost always cache hit
          # only runner.py changes rebuild the last layer (~5s)

  build-frontend:
    name: Build frontend
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.frontend == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
        working-directory: frontend
      - run: pnpm build
        working-directory: frontend
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: frontend-dist
          path: frontend/dist/
          retention-days: 1

  deploy:
    name: Deploy
    needs: [build-backend, build-sandbox, build-frontend]
    if: |
      always() &&
      !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') &&
      !contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') &&
      (
        needs.build-backend.result  == 'success' ||
        needs.build-sandbox.result  == 'success' ||
        needs.build-frontend.result == 'success'
      )
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Connect Tailscale
        uses: tailscale/github-action@v2
        with:
          oauth-client-id: ${{ secrets.TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID }}
          oauth-secret:    ${{ secrets.TS_OAUTH_SECRET }}
          tags: tag:ci

      - name: Setup SSH
        run: |
          echo "${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}" > /tmp/deploy_key
          chmod 600 /tmp/deploy_key

      - name: Upload compose files
        run: |
          scp -i /tmp/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            docker-compose.yml Caddyfile \
            ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}@${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:~/ml-toolbox/

      - name: Sync frontend
        if: needs.build-frontend.result == 'success'
        run: |
          rsync -az --delete -e "ssh -i /tmp/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" \
            frontend/dist/ \
            ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}@${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:~/ml-toolbox/frontend/dist/

      - name: Deploy containers
        if: |
          needs.build-backend.result == 'success' ||
          needs.build-sandbox.result == 'success'
        run: |
          ssh -i /tmp/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}@${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} << 'EOF'
            cd ~/ml-toolbox
            echo ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} | docker login ghcr.io -u ${{ github.actor }} --password-stdin
            docker compose pull
            docker compose up -d --no-build
            docker image prune -f
          EOF
```

### Secrets

```
TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID   ← Tailscale OAuth client ID  (set ✓)
TS_OAUTH_SECRET      ← Tailscale OAuth secret      (set ✓)
DEPLOY_SSH_KEY       ← ed25519 deploy key          (set ✓)
DEPLOY_USER          ← minyic                      (set ✓)
DEPLOY_HOST          ← minyis-mac-mini             (set ✓)
GITHUB_TOKEN         ← auto-provided by GHA, no setup needed
```

### Estimated times per scenario

| Scenario | CI | CD |
|---|---|---|
| Frontend only | both jobs ~75s | pnpm build + rsync ~15s (no container restart) |
| Backend logic only | both jobs ~75s | build-backend arm64 (cache hit) ~60s + deploy ~15s |
| runner.py only | both jobs ~75s | build-sandbox (last layer only) ~15s + deploy ~15s |
| Sandbox deps changed | both jobs ~75s | build-sandbox (Layer 2+) ~3-4min |
| Everything changed | both jobs parallel ~75s | three builds parallel + deploy |

self._running_container = container

            try:
                result_info = container.wait(timeout=300)
                if result_info["StatusCode"] != 0:
                    raise docker.errors.ContainerError(
                        container, result_info["StatusCode"],
                        None, "ml-toolbox-sandbox:latest",
                        container.logs(stderr=True),
                    )
            finally:
                self._running_container = None
                container.remove(force=True)

            result_path = run_dir / f"{node_id}_manifest_result.json"
            result = json.loads(result_path.read_text())

            # Translate container /data/... paths back to host paths
            host_result = {
                k: str(run_dir / Path(v).relative_to("/data"))
                for k, v in result.items()
            }
            (run_dir / f"{node_id}.hash").write_text(_params_hash(node))
            self._broadcast(node_id, "done", host_result)

        except docker.errors.ContainerError as e:
            self._broadcast(node_id, "error", traceback=e.stderr.decode())
            raise

    def _conditions_met(self, node_id: str, pipeline: Pipeline, run_dir: Path) -> bool:
        # eval() is fine here — expressions are written by the user themselves in the canvas,
        # and only read upstream output metadata (numbers like accuracy, loss).
        # Personal project, local network, single user — risk is effectively zero.
        for edge in pipeline.get_incoming_edges(node_id):
            if edge.condition:
                upstream_outputs = self._load_outputs(edge.source, run_dir)
                if not eval(edge.condition, {"__builtins__": {}}, {"outputs": upstream_outputs}):  # noqa: S307
                    return False
        return True

    def _topological_sort(self, pipeline: Pipeline) -> list[str]:
        # Kahn's algorithm — iterative, no recursion, no stack overflow risk
        in_degree: dict[str, int] = {n.id: 0 for n in pipeline.nodes}
        for edge in pipeline.edges:
            in_degree[edge.target] += 1
        queue = [nid for nid, deg in in_degree.items() if deg == 0]
        order: list[str] = []
        while queue:
            nid = queue.pop(0)
            order.append(nid)
            for edge in pipeline.edges:
                if edge.source == nid:
                    in_degree[edge.target] -= 1
                    if in_degree[edge.target] == 0:
                        queue.append(edge.target)
        return order
```

and reverse-proxies `/api` and `/ws` to the backend. Backend and sandbox are internal only.

Frontend is deployed by rsyncing `dist/` into `~/ml-toolbox/frontend/dist/` on the

**Security boundaries**:

| Attack vector | Blocked by |
|---|---|
| `os.system("rm -rf /")` | read-only root filesystem — only `/data` is writable |
| Network requests | `--network none` |
| Memory exhaustion | `--memory 1g` |
| Fork bomb | `--pids-limit 64` |
| Privilege escalation | `--cap-drop ALL` + `no-new-privileges` |
| Reading other pipelines | only current run dir is mounted |
| Infinite loop | 300s timeout |

### Graph Structure

- One or more root nodes (no inputs, e.g. FileInput)
- One or more leaf nodes (no outputs, e.g. ExportTable)
- Branching and merging allowed
- No cycles — enforced at edge creation time

## 1. Node Protocol

Every node — built-in or user-defined — uses the `@node` decorator.

```python
# ml_toolbox/protocol/decorators.py

@node(
    inputs={"df": TABLE},
    outputs={"df": TABLE},
    params={
        "strategy": Select(["mean", "median", "mode", "drop"], default="mean")
    }
)
def run(inputs: dict, params: dict) -> dict:
    """
    inputs:  {"df": "/data/node_abc.parquet"}   ← container-internal path
    params:  {"strategy": "mean"}
    returns: {"df": "/data/node_xyz.parquet"}
    """
    import polars as pl
    df = pl.read_parquet(inputs["df"])
    strategy = params["strategy"]
    if strategy == "drop":
        df = df.drop_nulls()
    else:
        df = df.fill_null(strategy=strategy)
    output_path = _get_output_path()
    df.write_parquet(output_path)
    return {"df": output_path}
```

### Port Types

```python
# ml_toolbox/protocol/types.py

class PortType(str, Enum):
    TABLE   = "TABLE"    # Polars DataFrame (stored as Parquet)
    MODEL   = "MODEL"    # any model object — sklearn, xgb, lgbm, torch (stored as pickle)
    METRICS = "METRICS"  # dict of evaluation results (stored as JSON)
    ARRAY   = "ARRAY"    # np.ndarray (stored as .npy)
    VALUE   = "VALUE"    # scalar: int / float / str / bool (stored as JSON)
    TENSOR  = "TENSOR"   # torch.Tensor / tf.Tensor (stored as .pt or .npy)

# Frontend port colors
PORT_COLORS = {
    "TABLE":   "#9CA3AF",  # grey
    "MODEL":   "#22C55E",  # green
    "METRICS": "#EAB308",  # yellow
    "ARRAY":   "#3B82F6",  # blue
    "VALUE":   "#A855F7",  # purple
    "TENSOR":  "#F97316",  # orange
}
```

Adding a new type = one line in the enum + one line in PORT_COLORS. No other changes required.

### Param Types

```python
# ml_toolbox/protocol/params.py

Select(options: list, default=None)        # dropdown
Slider(min: float, max: float, step: float, default: float)  # range input
Text(default: str = "")                    # free text
Toggle(default: bool = False)              # boolean checkbox
```

### Node Metadata

The `@node` decorator registers metadata exposed to the frontend via API:

```json
{
  "id": "clean",
  "name": "Clean",
  "inputs":  [{"name": "df", "type": "TABLE"}],
  "outputs": [{"name": "df", "type": "TABLE"}],
  "params":  [
    {
      "name": "strategy",
      "type": "select",
      "options": ["mean", "median", "mode", "drop"],
      "default": "mean"
    }
  ],
  "code": "def run(inputs, params):\n    ..."
}
```

## 2. Backend API (REST)

All canvas operations have API support. The frontend is a UI layer on top of these
endpoints — everything can also be driven by curl or scripts.

```
# Node Library
GET    /api/nodes                                             → list all registered node definitions
GET    /api/nodes/{node_type}                                 → single node definition + default code

# Pipeline CRUD
POST   /api/pipelines                                         → create pipeline, returns {id, name}
GET    /api/pipelines                                         → list all pipelines
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}                                    → load pipeline (nodes + edges + settings)
PUT    /api/pipelines/{id}                                    → save pipeline (full state replace)
DELETE /api/pipelines/{id}                                    → delete pipeline
POST   /api/pipelines/{id}/duplicate                          → clone pipeline, returns {id, name}

# Node operations
POST   /api/pipelines/{id}/nodes                              → add node {type, position, params?}
DELETE /api/pipelines/{id}/nodes/{node_id}                    → remove node + its edges
PATCH  /api/pipelines/{id}/nodes/{node_id}                    → update params / code / position (partial)

# Edge operations
POST   /api/pipelines/{id}/edges                              → connect two ports {source, source_port, target, target_port}
DELETE /api/pipelines/{id}/edges/{edge_id}                    → remove edge
PATCH  /api/pipelines/{id}/edges/{edge_id}                    → update condition

# Pipeline settings
PATCH  /api/pipelines/{id}/settings                           → update settings (e.g. keep_outputs)

# Execution
POST   /api/pipelines/{id}/run                                → run full pipeline (new run_id)
POST   /api/pipelines/{id}/run/{node_id}                      → re-run node + all downstream (new run_id)
POST   /api/pipelines/{id}/cancel                             → stop running execution
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}/status                             → {is_running, current_node_id, last_run_id}

# Run history
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}/runs                               → list runs [{run_id, created_at, status}]
DELETE /api/pipelines/{id}/runs/{run_id}                      → delete run directory

# Node outputs  (run_id optional — defaults to latest run)
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}/outputs/{node_id}?run_id=          → output metadata + chart PNGs
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}/outputs/{node_id}/download?run_id= → download output file
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}/runs/{run_id}/outputs/{node_id}          → output from specific run
GET    /api/pipelines/{id}/runs/{run_id}/outputs/{node_id}/download → download from specific run

# WebSocket
WS     /ws/pipelines/{id}                                     → real-time execution status per node
```

{
  "id": "uuid",
  "name": "My Pipeline",
  "settings": { "keep_outputs": true },
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "instance_uuid",
      "type": "clean",

  "outputs": {"df": "/tmp/..."},

}
```

## 3. Execution Engine

See full implementation in Core Design Decisions → Node Execution Sandbox above.

### Cycle Detection

At edge creation time (frontend + backend validation):

```python
def would_create_cycle(pipeline, source_id, target_id) -> bool:
    # DFS from target — if we can reach source, adding this edge creates a cycle
    visited = set()
    stack = [target_id]
    while stack:
        node = stack.pop()
        if node == source_id:
            return True
        if node not in visited:
            visited.add(node)
            stack.extend(pipeline.get_downstream(node))
    return False
```

---

## 4. Frontend — Canvas

### Canvas Features

```
- Sidebar: node library panel (drag node onto canvas to add)
- Drag nodes to reposition
- Click output port → drag edge → drop on input port to connect
- Port type validation: mismatched types → red flash, connection rejected
- Cycle detection: would-create-cycle → connection rejected
- Click edge → shows condition input field mid-edge
- Click node → opens right panel
- Right-click node → context menu: rename / duplicate / delete / run
- Delete key → removes selected node or edge
- Canvas: pan (drag background) + zoom (scroll)
- Mini-map bottom-right
- Keyboard: Ctrl+Z undo, Ctrl+Shift+Z redo
```

### Node Card (React Flow custom node)

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  Clean Data            ●     │  status dot: grey/blue/green/red
│  ─────────────────────────   │
│  ○ df          TABLE         │  input port (left), coloured by type
│                   TABLE  ○   │  output port (right), coloured by type
│  ─────────────────────────   │
│  [Params]  [Code]  [Output]  │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

Styles:

border:         1px solid #E5E7EB
border-radius:  8px
selected:       2px solid #3B82F6

### Right Panel (three tabs)

**Params tab**
- Auto-rendered from node param definitions

- `Slider`  → range input + number display
- `Text`    → text input
- `Toggle`  → checkbox

**Code tab**
- Monaco Editor, Python syntax highlighting
- Dark theme inside panel (contrast against light card UI)
- Auto-save on blur → `PUT /api/pipelines/{id}`

import json
from pathlib import Path

import polars as pl

from ml_toolbox.protocol import PortType, Select, Slider, node


def _get_output_path() -> Path:
    """Return the output directory for node artifacts.

    At runtime this is overridden by the sandbox runner to point at the
    container's scratch volume.  During development / tests it falls back
    to a temp-style local path.
    """
    p = Path("/tmp/ml_toolbox_outputs")
    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    return p


# ── generate_data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
@node(
    outputs={"df": PortType.TABLE},
    params={"rows": Slider(min=10, max=1000, step=10, default=100)},
)
def run(inputs: dict, params: dict) -> dict:  # noqa: ARG001
    """Generate a random DataFrame and write it to parquet."""
    import random

    n = int(params.get("rows", 100))
    df = pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "id": list(range(n)),
            "value_a": [random.gauss(0, 1) for _ in range(n)],
            "value_b": [random.gauss(5, 2) for _ in range(n)],
            "category": [random.choice(["A", "B", "C"]) for _ in range(n)],
        }
    )
    out = _get_output_path() / "generate_data.parquet"
    df.write_parquet(out)
    return {"df": str(out)}


# ── clean_data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
@node(
    inputs={"df": PortType.TABLE},
    outputs={"df": PortType.TABLE},
    params={
        "strategy": Select(["mean", "median", "drop"], default="mean"),
    },
)
def clean_data(inputs: dict, params: dict) -> dict:
    """Read parquet, handle nulls according to strategy, write parquet."""
    df = pl.read_parquet(inputs["df"])
    strategy = params.get("strategy", "mean")

    if strategy == "drop":
        df = df.drop_nulls()
    else:
        numeric_cols = [c for c in df.columns if df[c].dtype.is_numeric()]
        for col in numeric_cols:
            fill_value = getattr(df[col], strategy)()
            df = df.with_columns(pl.col(col).fill_null(fill_value))

    out = _get_output_path() / "clean_data.parquet"
    df.write_parquet(out)
    return {"df": str(out)}


# ── summarize_data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
@node(
    inputs={"df": PortType.TABLE},
    outputs={"summary": PortType.METRICS},
)
def summarize_data(inputs: dict, params: dict) -> dict:  # noqa: ARG001
    """Read parquet and compute basic statistics."""
    df = pl.read_parquet(inputs["df"])

    summary = {
        "row_count": df.height,
        "column_count": df.width,
        "null_counts": {col: df[col].null_count() for col in df.columns},
    }

    out = _get_output_path() / "summarize_data.json"
    out.write_text(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
    return {"summary": str(out)}

"""Tests for the node protocol, decorator, and demo nodes."""

from pathlib import Path
from unittest.mock import patch

import polars as pl

from ml_toolbox.protocol import NODE_REGISTRY


# Import nodes to trigger registration
import ml_toolbox.nodes  # noqa: F401


EXPECTED_NODES = {
    "ml_toolbox.nodes.demo.run",
    "ml_toolbox.nodes.demo.clean_data",
    "ml_toolbox.nodes.demo.summarize_data",
}


def test_registry_contains_all_demo_nodes():
    """All 3 demo nodes should be registered after import."""
    assert EXPECTED_NODES.issubset(NODE_REGISTRY.keys())


def test_generate_data_metadata():
    meta = NODE_REGISTRY["ml_toolbox.nodes.demo.run"]
    assert meta["name"] == "run"
    assert meta["inputs"] == []
    assert meta["outputs"] == [{"name": "df", "type": "TABLE"}]
    assert len(meta["params"]) == 1
    param = meta["params"][0]
    assert param["type"] == "slider"
    assert param["name"] == "rows"
    assert param["min"] == 10
    assert param["max"] == 1000
    assert "code" in meta


def test_clean_data_metadata():
    meta = NODE_REGISTRY["ml_toolbox.nodes.demo.clean_data"]
    assert meta["inputs"] == [{"name": "df", "type": "TABLE"}]
    assert meta["outputs"] == [{"name": "df", "type": "TABLE"}]
    assert len(meta["params"]) == 1
    param = meta["params"][0]
    assert param["type"] == "select"
    assert param["name"] == "strategy"
    assert "mean" in param["options"]


def test_summarize_data_metadata():
    meta = NODE_REGISTRY["ml_toolbox.nodes.demo.summarize_data"]
    assert meta["inputs"] == [{"name": "df", "type": "TABLE"}]
    assert meta["outputs"] == [{"name": "summary", "type": "METRICS"}]
    assert meta["params"] == []


def test_generate_data_produces_parquet(tmp_path: Path):
    """Running generate_data should produce a valid parquet file."""
    from ml_toolbox.nodes.demo import run as generate_data

    with patch(
        "ml_toolbox.nodes.demo._get_output_path", return_value=tmp_path
    ):
        result = generate_data(inputs={}, params={"rows": 50})

    parquet_path = Path(result["df"])
    assert parquet_path.exists()
    df = pl.read_parquet(parquet_path)
    assert df.height == 50
    assert set(df.columns) == {"id", "value_a", "value_b", "category"}
